page.content: The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive overview of the gender distribution within the surveyed population, highlighting the significant representation of both males and females. Of the total 7,444 individuals accounted for, males constitute 39.3%, amounting to 2,924 participants, while females represent a larger segment at 60.7%, totaling 4,520 individuals. This disparity in gender representation underscores the importance of considering demographic factors when analyzing the results of the survey.

lines.text: The data presented in the table offers a comprehensive overview of the gender distribution within the surveyed population,
highlighting the significant representation of both males and females. Of the total 7,444 individuals accounted for, males
constitute 39.3%, amounting to 2,924 participants, while females represent a larger segment at 60.7%, totaling 4,520
individuals. This disparity in gender representation underscores the importance of considering demographic factors when
analyzing the results of the survey.

page.content: The predominance of female respondents may reflect broader societal trends or specific characteristics of the surveyed population, which could influence the outcomes of the study. Understanding the implications of this gender distribution is crucial for interpreting the data accurately and offering insights that are relevant to both male and female experiences. Furthermore, such demographic insights can inform future research methodologies and participant recruitment strategies, ensuring that they achieve a more balanced representation. The subsequent analysis will delve deeper into the implications of these findings, aiming to elucidate the potential impacts that gender dynamics may have on the overall results and conclusions drawn from this data set.
